description: LROC curated Land Rover VIN and pre-VIN chassis decoder data. Values are model-root aware and should prefer unknown over guessed meanings.

vin17.usage_note: 17-character Land Rover VINs are decoded by their first five characters where possible. For Land Rover/UK-ROW VINs the 10th character uses Land Rover-specific model-year/service-period tables. Around 1996-2000 Land Rover uses T, V, W, X, Y, then numeric 1-9 for 2001-2009, then A onwards from 2010 (L = 2020). These codes are not the same as generic North American VIN tables or UK registration plate age letters. Land Rover reused some model-year codes across production cycles; decade hints can narrow ambiguous results.

root.label: Model line
root.meaning: Land Rover Ninety / One Ten / 127 / 130 / Defender family

notes: This root covers the coil-sprung Land Rover utility family, including Ninety, One Ten, 127/130 and Defender-era vehicles.
Some code meanings vary by model/service period and market; unknown codes should be left unknown rather than guessed.

A.meaning: 88 inch / 90 inch short wheelbase, depending on model period

C.meaning: 109 inch / long wheelbase utility, depending on model period

A.meaning: Truck cab, soft top, hard top, or utility body, depending on model period

B.meaning: 3-door station wagon / hard top, depending on model period

C.meaning: Pick-up / cab chassis, depending on model period

E.meaning: 2-door crew cab, depending on model period

V.meaning: County / station wagon, depending on model period

W.meaning: Station wagon, depending on model period

notes: Often associated with pre-Tdi turbo diesel vehicles. Confirm against engine number and records.

F.meaning: 200Tdi or 300Tdi diesel, depending on model/service period

period_overrides.meaning: 2.5 litre Tdi diesel family; around the 1994 transition period this may be 200Tdi or 300Tdi territory. Confirm against engine number and vehicle records.
2.5 litre 300Tdi diesel family, subject to market and emissions specification.

G.meaning: 2.25 litre diesel / other period-specific diesel usage

P.meaning: Puma diesel family, later Defender period

1.meaning: Right-hand drive (RHD), 4- or 5-speed manual, depending on model period

2.meaning: Left-hand drive (LHD), 4- or 5-speed manual, depending on model period

10.label: Land Rover model year / service period

notes: Early Land Rover VIN usage before the later all-model year-code sequence. Confirm against model line and factory records.

pre_vin_chassis.usage_note: Pre-VIN Land Rover chassis numbers are decoded when a matching local Series pattern or prefix range is available. Older plates can be moved or replaced, so the checker should be treated as a guide, not proof of identity.

notes: LRSOC is treated as the primary Series I chassis-numbering source for these ranges.
1949 changed to 7 digits and introduced the third digit for sub-model; 1950 changed to 8 digits with the first digit designating model year.
For 1951-1953 LRSOC lists separate Home, RHD export, LHD export and CKD ranges where applicable.
CKD means Completely Knocked Down: the vehicle was despatched unassembled and assembled by the importer or local dealer.
caveats: The decode is range-based from LRSOC and should be treated as model/service period guidance, not proof of exact build date.
For rare bodies such as Tickford Station Wagon or Mobile Welder, confirm identity against club or heritage records.

patterns.name: Series I 1954-1955 86/107 chassis number
patterns.description: LRSOC/Rover 1954-1955 Series I 86 inch and 107 inch 8-digit chassis numbering structure.
notes: LRSOC lists the 1954/1955 system as first digit model year, second digit 7 for Land Rover, third digit model/wheelbase, and fourth digit specification/market.
This is a structural decode because LRSOC publishes the digit meanings rather than a full from/to table for every 1954/1955 variant on this page.
86 inch Station Wagons did not have special numbering; LWB Station Wagons remained 107 inch in this era.

patterns.name: Series I 1956 86/107 chassis number
patterns.description: LRSOC/Rover 1956 Series I 86 inch, 107 inch, and 107 Station Wagon 9-digit chassis numbering structure.

notes: A vehicle can have been physically built in late 1955 while decoding as the 1956 model/service period.
LRSOC says Rover moved to a 9-digit sequence for 1956, pre-empting the 88 inch and 109 inch models.
This is a structural decode for the 86 inch, 107 inch and 107 Station Wagon sequence.
This Series I rule is kept separate from the later Series II/IIA/III prefix decoder.

patterns.name: Series I 1956-1958 88/109 chassis number
patterns.description: LRSOC/Rover 1956-1958 Series I 88 inch, 109 inch, diesel and station wagon 9-digit chassis numbering structure.
notes: LRSOC lists second-and-third digit variants for 88 inch, 109 inch, diesel, CKD and Station Wagon Series I vehicles for 1956-1958.
This is a structural decode where LRSOC gives variant codes rather than a complete from/to serial range table on this page.
The 88 inch wheelbase was introduced during this period; confirm exact build date from authoritative records.

patterns.name: Series II / IIA / III pre-VIN chassis prefix decoder
patterns.description: Decodes Land Rover Series II/IIA/III and related pre-1980 prefix ranges using the first three digits, optional suffix letter, and remaining serial number.

Early Range Rover pre-VIN chassis number family

A.period: First major suffix/revision period; commonly early-to-mid 1960s for Series IIA context

H.period: Series III / late Series transition context; confirm from vehicle records

caveats: From 1962 to 1979 the first three digits identify the model/specification range; the chassis number itself normally does not give an exact build year.
Suffix letters indicate design/modification states rather than a precise model year. Use registration records, compliance plates or a heritage certificate for exact build information.
Some prefix ranges overlap broad reference groups; specific ranges are preferred before broad fallback ranges.

source_notes: Where LRSOC lists an R/L prefix, the checker decodes that prefix when supplied and warns when the range is known but the prefix is omitted.
LRSOC explicit from/to ranges are used for Series I 1948-1953 before falling back to later structural Series I rules.
Reference structure: pre-1980 Land Rover chassis numbers vary from six to nine digits; the 1962-1979 system uses first-three-digit model/specification prefixes and optional suffix letters.
The local table deliberately decodes ranges conservatively and returns unknown when a prefix is not in the curated table.
